Alright, welcome back to our next lesson on customers for life. I want to focus now on the customer path, meaning how do you take that customer who you just closed and we know about now that first upsell or cross sell, but how do you fully develop this customer over the long term? Well, it starts with a phrase that was coined by my good friend, not in real life, but in history, Sun Tzu, the art of war, a man I greatly respect. His saying that I love is every battle is one before it's ever fought. Now, on many levels, the straight line system was designed with that specific outcome in mind. In other words, it's a backwards looking system. We say to ourselves, what has to line up in the prospect's mind? What does he need to hear and in what order for him to feel comfortable enough to say? Yes, that's how it basically works. But now let's look at that same idea of every battle is one before it's ever fought. Understand that when it's not a battle, we're not at odds, so to speak. No one's getting hurt. It's not like a victory and a loser. It's a two sided victory here. But still, what I'm referring to here is the idea that you have to have a customer pathway mapped out in your own mind before you start the process of just randomly upselling someone and cross selling them. What is that best pathway that fits most of your customers needs? In other words, you start by having this overarching pathway. What you represent your perfect customer, all the different things he would buy. What's the best order? What serves the customer best? Put the customer first. In what order you're going to make the sales? Typically, in other words, you wouldn't start off with the most expensive sale first. In fact, online typically you start with a low priced over a chip wire offer as it's called. Right. And then use that to generate a first time client. And then from there, you would take that new client through your process of cold day meant to develop a new custom for life with the second step now being perhaps setting up a free strategy session or a coaching call. Well, you know, actually get on the phone with this new client who so far has only been through a marketing phone. In other words, they might have bought this seven or 20 hour product online. That's your trip wire offer. And now that they are in your so-called system as a paying client, you want to call them back. Get on the phone and find out more about them through a straight line intelligence gathering session, find out what their needs are, what their pain points are and so forth. And then based on that, what you want to have is a customer pathway that you've already sort of thought out beforehand, but there's little variations based on each person. And then you want to put that person into the pathway that best suits their needs. In other words, you don't want to start off flying blind. You want to know what you're going to sell them. First, second, third, fourth and fifth, understanding that it's obviously not this perfect formula where everyone's going to buy the same thing or else there would be no customization and you're really not acting in your prospect's best interest. But you want to have sort of options, pathways that serve as these guiding lights to the promised land, which is a fully developed client who buys for you again and again and again, hopefully a subscription model where there's renewals involved and they stay with you forever. That's the way you should work this in terms of developing a client. So again, you start off with this trip wire offer and you want to get on the phone with them. And by the way, you don't necessarily need to do that with that next step. You actually can sell them something else up to about a thousand dollars after that. Really, you guess you can sell them a higher price product, but really above that, it's much more effective to get on the phone and speak to them, develop this relationship. That's why I always say when it comes to online marketers, this crazy belief that some of them have, I just perfect my online marketing. I'll never have to speak to a human being. You can make a bit of money, but the great ones, the ones that really know their stuff, the true experts, they use the best of both worlds. They take online marketing. They combine it with online sales closes to bring in all of these new clients at a relatively low price point, but now they have them in their system as paying clients. From there, they can go into the sales department who has these super hot leads, not even leads their clients, but they're almost like these raw leads are already paying. Wow. And you set up these strategy sessions or these calls where you identify their needs, their values, their pain points, their beliefs, and then you tailor this customer pathway based on the four or five ones that you have. It's sort of like, you know, your overarching pathways and you find the one that best fits this person and bam, that's how you truly go about taking this new customer and fully monetizing them while giving them massive value at the same time. Remember very often in business, no money is made on this first sale. You understand? It's about how much money you can make over the long term by providing a custom with value again and again and again. Cause once that customer is yours, the marketing costs dropped to almost zero. Right? You did the marketing is done now. So it's much less expensive. In fact, the cost of a new client in terms of the revenue that's derived from that is seven to 800% higher than the cost of revenue derived from an existing client. So how do you go about doing that? Well, in the same way that you have these strategies for upselling, cross-selling, you also want to make sure that you're providing periodic check-ins with these clients and that you're well aware of their consistent usage of what applications they're using and you can have certain features and benefits to your particular product that are really called the stickiest means they, the ones that kind of people like and once they use them, they have a pain of disconnect. So they don't want to leave you once they use these, make sure that your new clients are using the stickier products and then also make sure that before the subscription is going to expire, you want to actually reach out to them. How long before it starts 120 days before? In other words, don't wait until June 30th to call them if they expire on July 1. You want to start that one quarter before that and already start the process of making sure that they're happy, that they're seeing value of product. They're using anything that fits that needs to be there. There's a lot of things they're not using right now that you need a bit of a push. Get them using your product. Make sure they're engaging at a high level. I promise you those renewals will spike dramatically. All right. And I got to say, I can go on and on here, but you got the point. The key here is to understand that after you close the deal, that's when things truly begin by giving excellent customer service, by focusing on your onboarding, by focusing on adoption, not leaving it up the chest, not just crossing your fingers and customer service, customer success, got this. No. Make sure the handoff is being done correctly. So if there's a handoff, I want you on that call if possible, or at least sitting in that thank you note. And then from there, make sure that you stay with this customer and keep providing value to them in the form of advice, service, periodic follow-up calls, solving their problems, check in with them randomly just for no reason at all. And once in a while to show that you care, you do that. You're a huge asset to the company you work for, and you're making a ton of money as well. Bottom line. All right. So with that, two things left here before we move on to a new module. Number one, I want to go through obviously your referral system. I'm going to hand this to you in a silver platter. Got a great system for generating massive referrals. And then after that, I'm going to sum all this up and give you five simple steps, some I mentioned before, five things to just deploy on a consistent basis, its mindset and strategy wrapped up together to make sure that you're always maximizing each new customer and truly building those all important customers for life. So take your quiz right now, go for your exercise, and I will see you very soon.
